title: gambelia glabrata
intro: Gambelia glabrata, also known as the desert marigold or sticky gilia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and aiding in the healing of wounds. Gambelia glabrata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
